mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in wages and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index over the past 6 months signals an increase in supply chain activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-production periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to a decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is amplified when the Unemployment Rate is below 5%.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-interest-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the number of Housing Starts over the past 6 months signals an increase in housing demand, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This effect is amplified when the Consumer Price Index is below 2%.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-housing-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Unemployment Rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in wages and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is amplified when the Industrial Production Index is above 2%.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the federal funds rate over the past 6 months signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This effect is amplified when the Consumer Price Index is above 2%.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-interest-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
